name: Udmurtia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the cosmos, purity and the moral law

description: A vertical triband of black, white and red with an eight-pointed solar sign (an octagonal star) in red on the central white stripe.
history: The flag of the Udmurt Republic was adopted on 4 November 1993. The eight-pointed star is the traditional Udmurt solar amulet, believed to protect against evil; the colour scheme reflects ancient Finno-Ugric cosmology.

features.symbolism: the black, white and red tricolour stands for the earth, the cosmic order and the sun and life respectively, drawn from traditional Udmurt cosmology.
the eight-pointed star is the traditional Udmurt solar sign, a guardian symbol against misfortune; placed on the central white stripe in red.
